title: MSPs to vote on banning US troops from Scottish airports

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Scottish Greens co-leader Gillian Mackay MSP has called on the Scottish Government to evict US military forces from the publicly-owned Glasgow…

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: US military flights have landed at Prestwick Airport over 550 times since April 1 2025 (Image: Ben Birchall/PA) This article is brought to you by our exclusive subscriber partnership with our sister title USA Today, and has been written by our American colleagues. It does not necessarily reflect the view of The Herald.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: MSPs are to vote on whether to evict US military forces from Prestwick Airport, following continued military activity and reports of breaches of Scottish jurisdiction. The Scottish Greens are to use a parliamentary debate on Wednesday to call on the US military to be banned from using Prestwick and other publicly owned airports in Scotland.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – MSPs to vote on banning Donald Trump's troops from using Scottish airports
